title: Daily Market Digest: March 24, 2026
subtitle: Market sentiment mixed as geopolitical tensions persist and economic data is awaited. Not financial advice.

items.headline: U.S. Futures Slip Amid Fading Hopes for Iran De-escalation
items.summary: U.S. stock futures edged lower on Tuesday as hopes for an immediate resolution to the U.S.-Iran standoff waned after Iran denied President Trump's claims of productive talks. This follows a strong rally on Monday driven by initial optimism. Investors are also digesting mixed global growth signals and persistent cost pressures.

items.headline: Applied Optoelectronics Surges on Major Order
items.summary: Applied Optoelectronics Inc. ($AAOI) jumped 4.16% in premarket trading after securing an initial volume order exceeding $53 million for 800G single-mode data center transceivers.

items.headline: Fabrinet Gains on Strong Operating Performance
items.summary: Fabrinet ($FN) rose 7.94% following positive commentary regarding its operating performance.

items.headline: Albemarle Continues Upward Trend
items.summary: Albemarle ($ALB) saw a gain of 6.93% in early trading, building on its 3.5% jump from Monday.

items.headline: Estée Lauder Drops on Potential Business Combination Talks
items.summary: Estée Lauder Companies ($EL) declined 7.72% after confirming it is in talks for a potential business combination with Puig Brands.

items.headline: Bloom Energy Experiences Decline
items.summary: Bloom Energy ($BE) fell 5.86% in early trading.

items.headline: Fair Isaac Slips
items.summary: Fair Isaac ($FICO) saw a decrease of 5.70% this morning.

items.headline: Iran Tensions Fuel Oil Price Swings and Market Volatility
items.summary: Geopolitical tensions surrounding the U.S.-Iran conflict continue to drive market volatility and have led to rapid increases in oil prices. While hopes of de-escalation on Monday caused crude oil prices to fall sharply, futures were trading higher today as Iran denied talks. Brent crude also clawed back losses to top $100 after hours Monday.

items.headline: Fed's Rate Cut Plans Under Scrutiny Amid Inflation Surge
items.summary: The Federal Reserve's monetary policy outlook appears to be shifting, with rising oil prices and persistent inflation concerns putting planned rate cuts into question. The Cleveland Fed's Inflation Nowcasting tool projects the Consumer Price Index (CPI) to surge to 3.02% in March, up from 2.4% in February. The probability of a rate hike in April is now 8.3%, up from effectively 0% just weeks ago.

items.headline: Consumer Discretionary, Materials, and Tech Lead Monday's Rally, Energy Sector Remains Strong
items.summary: On Monday, all 11 S&P 500 sectors closed in positive territory, with Consumer Discretionary (+2.5%), Materials (+1.5%), and Information Technology (+1.5%) leading the gains. The energy sector continues to show strength, while information technology, consumer discretionary, and financials remain below their highs due to various concerns including AI spending, tariffs, rising oil prices, and private credit market stress.

items.headline: Key Economic Data and Earnings Reports Awaited Today
items.summary: Investors are awaiting U.S. manufacturing data today. Several major companies are scheduled to release their quarterly earnings reports, including GameStop ($GME), KB Home ($KBH), and Smithfield Foods ($SFD).
